title: pogostemon cablin
intro: Pogostemon cablin, also known as patchouli, is a perennial herb native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its small purple flowers and distinctive, earthy aroma. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, primarily for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of patchouli include reducing stress and anxiety, improving skin health, and supporting digestive well-being. Patchouli is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
